Acceptance Criteria: Category selected, likely Technology or Design, with hardware/product rules understood

Task: Define campaign goal in one sentence
Acceptance Criteria: Clear promise explains what Pixora is, who it is for, and what backers receive
At least 3 personas documented: smart home users, makers, desk/dashboard fans, small businesses
Launch date and time chosen, with at least 6 weeks of prep before launch
Daily/weekly plan for prelaunch, launch day, mid-campaign, final 48 hours, and post-campaign
Goal includes BOM, manufacturing, packaging, shipping buffer, taxes, Kickstarter fees, payment fees, replacement units, and contingency
Minimum unit count covers supplier MOQs and still leaves margin after fees and fulfillment
Decision made whether to avoid stretch goals or only offer low-risk software/card-library stretch goals
Account exists and can access project creator tools
Creator or business verification submitted and accepted
Bank account is in the creator/entity name and verified before campaign review
Valid card attached in creator/entity name
Legal/tax path chosen before entering payout details
Expected tax reporting and sales tax/VAT responsibilities documented with accountant if needed
Exact display size, enclosure, power, mounting/stand, included accessories, and firmware version documented
Campaign promises limited to features that are already working or low-risk to finish
No-subscription, local server expectations, cloud limitations, mobile app status, integrations, and supported devices clarified
List of cards included at launch: clock, weather, NASA/SpaceX, Etsy, sports, Home Assistant/Hubitat, stocks, travel, social, etc.
Clear requirements for Windows/local server, Wi-Fi, power, and account/API keys for optional integrations
One polished prototype runs reliably for video and photos
Second working unit available in case the hero unit fails during filming or live demo
Campaign media shows real hardware operating, not only renders or mockups
Video clip shows connecting a display, configuring Wi-Fi, adding cards, and seeing the display update
Video clip shows at least two displays working together for wall/test animations
Prototype runs a mixed card deck for 72 hours without server or display failure
Complete bill of materials with supplier, part number, unit cost, lead time, and backup source
Unit economics calculated at 50 units
Unit economics calculated at 100 units
Unit economics calculated at 250 units
Decision made: 3D printed, acrylic, off-the-shelf, CNC, injection mold, or hybrid
Per-unit enclosure cost and lead time confirmed with supplier or internal process
Who assembles, flashes, tests, packs, and labels each unit is documented
Per-unit test steps include display pixels, Wi-Fi, server connection, color order, firmware version, and burn-in
Top 10 supply/assembly risks listed with mitigation plans
FCC/CE/UL/power-supply responsibilities documented for the exact reward configuration
Included/recommended power supply certification status confirmed
Wi-Fi module certification and labeling implications checked
All bundled fonts, libraries, firmware, and card code licenses reviewed for redistribution
Power, heat, indoor-use, mounting, and child-safety guidance drafted
Hardware, enclosure, packaging, labor, fees, shipping buffer, replacements, and tax reserve included
Early bird price covers cost with acceptable margin and limited quantity
Standard reward price covers expected worst-case unit cost and fulfillment
Two-pack price accounts for shipping weight, packaging, and assembly time
Only low-risk add-ons included, such as extra stand, cable, alternate case color, or wall kit
3-10 tiers defined with title, price, contents, quantity limit, delivery date, and shipping rules
Low-price support tier created with realistic non-physical reward
Limited quantity tier written and priced
Main featured reward tier written and priced
Two-pack or small-business tier written and priced if economics work
Delivery dates include manufacturing, compliance, shipping, and contingency
Box dimensions and weight measured with realistic packaging
US shipping cost estimated with carrier/service and buffer
International regions either priced realistically or excluded intentionally
Decision made whether to collect shipping on Kickstarter or later via pledge manager
Replacement budget and policy documented
Self-fulfillment, 3PL, or hybrid chosen with cost estimate
Title includes Pixora and a searchable phrase like LED dashboard or smart display
Short blurb clearly explains Pixora in under 135 characters
Image looks good at thumbnail size and with play button overlay
Page includes problem, solution, product tour, use cases, specs, rewards, timeline, risks, and team
Honest risks section covers hardware supply, compliance, software polish, shipping, and timeline
FAQ answers setup, subscriptions, integrations, privacy/local-first, shipping, warranty, and compatibility
At least 8 high-quality photos of real device, app, cards, packaging/prototype, and scale/context
Display size, resolution, power, connectivity, supported OS/server, enclosure dimensions, included items
60-120 second script covers hook, product demo, why now, rewards, and call to action
Shot list includes real LED display, app setup, card library, group wall, integrations, and close-ups
Clean footage captured with readable LED matrix and no flicker issues
Short authentic founder clip explaining why Pixora exists
Final video uploaded early to verify Kickstarter encoding
Captions or text overlays added for silent viewing
Tagline, pronunciation, noun line, and brand language consistent across page/video/app
Logo, colors, product images, and screenshots consistent and polished
Folder includes logo, product photos, short description, founder bio, and contact info
Email capture page live with product image, value prop, and Kickstarter follow link when available
Target number of qualified emails set and tracked before launch
Friends/family, smart-home communities, maker groups, local press, YouTube/TikTok creators, newsletters listed
Launch-day email written and tested
Mid-campaign and final-48-hour emails written
At least 20 posts/clips/images prepared for launch month
List of 20 relevant reviewers/creators with contact status and sample unit decision
Stable version chosen for campaign video and prototype demos
New user can configure server, Wi-Fi, and first card without help
Public list of included cards and integrations is accurate
How firmware/app/card updates reach backers after delivery is documented
Local-first behavior, API key storage, and network access are explained plainly
Known bugs, unsupported integrations, and rough edges are documented internally
Printable or web quick-start draft complete
Wi-Fi, server not found, blank display, color order, API keys, and firmware update fixes documented
Email/form/process for backer support chosen
Policy covers DOA, defects, shipping damage, and user damage boundaries
Line-item budget with best/base/worst case and funding goal calculation

Task: Add Kickstarter and Stripe fee buffer
Acceptance Criteria: Budget includes 5% Kickstarter and 3-5% payment processing
Budget includes dropped pledges and failed payments assumption
At least 10-20% contingency modeled for hardware surprises
Tax reserve estimated with professional guidance if possible
Preview link sent to trusted reviewers for copy, pricing, and clarity feedback
Every link, image, reward, delivery date, shipping rule, and typo checked
Continue/review step completed at least two business days before launch
Bank verified, video encoded, rewards locked, outreach scheduled, support inbox ready
